subject: Weekly AI Research Roundup
clean_text:
This week researchers at ETH Zurich released LongBench-Recall, a benchmark that measures how well long-context language models retrieve facts buried deep in their input. The benchmark covers 12 tasks ranging from 8k to 128k tokens, and the team reports that most production models drop sharply past 64k tokens. They have open-sourced the evaluation harness and a starter leaderboard on GitHub.

subject: Frontier Model Notes
clean_text:
A new mixture-of-experts model with 8 active experts out of 64 was published this week. The authors report matching a dense 70B model's quality at roughly a quarter of the inference cost, with routing stability improvements that cut expert collapse incidents by 40 percent. Code and weights are released under an Apache license.

subject: Open Source Watch
clean_text:
The llms.cpp project ported a small transformer to a single C++ header this week, with no dependencies beyond the standard library. Benchmarks on a laptop CPU show 18 tokens per second for a 1.3B model, and the maintainer is collecting community benchmarks through a simple JSON submission script.

subject: Build a Local RAG Index
clean_text: This issue walks through building a local RAG index from your own notes. The instructions cover installing the vector library, chunking your markdown files, and running the indexer overnight. Following the instructions carefully avoids the most common pitfalls, and the instructions include a troubleshooting section for when embeddings fail silently. Readers who skip the instructions tend to hit indexing errors within the first hundred documents.
